title: Teyana Taylor Wins Golden Globe for Best Supporting Actress
text: Teyana Taylor claimed her first Golden Globe award at the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony, taking home the prize for best supporting actress in a motion picture. She earned the recognition for her role as Perfidia Beverly Hills in Paul Thomas Anderson's film "One Battle After Another." Taylor became visibly emotional upon hearing her name announced by presenters Amanda Seyfried and Jennifer Garner. During her acceptance remarks, she addressed her daughters watching from upstairs at the Beverly Hilton, humorously telling them to put away their phones. She also praised director Anderson, saying: "Thank you for your vision, your trust and your brilliance." Taylor concluded her speech with an inspiring statement directed at women of color. She emphasized: "Our softness is not a liability. Our depth is not too much. Our light does not need permission to shine. We belong in every room we walk into." Other nominees in the category included Amy Madigan, Elle Fanning, Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas, Emily Blunt, and Ariana Grande, making Taylor's win particularly significant as her first Golden Globe nomination.
